behavior_summary: This script implements the in-game behaviour for a grenade-like item. It provides a read-only 'U8P_FGRENADE::look' description depending on the item's frame/variant, and a 'U8P_FGRENADE::use' process that arms and activates the grenade producing different effects depending on the variant (animation + explosion, a special-case handler, or a multi-fragment "chaos" spread).

The 'U8P_FGRENADE::use' routine prevents reactivation via an item-level Q flag (acts as an arm/one-shot guard), then branches on Item::getFrame to run variant-specific logic: an animation loop that steps frames then explodes, a special-case call (METHOD::func0676) for one frame value, or repeated spawn-and-hurl of fragments plus igniteChaos for the chaos variant. All activation branches end by playing SFX, creating explosion sprite(s), and destroying the item.
U8P_FGRENADE::hit and U8P_FGRENADE::gotHit are present as hooks (signatures accept uword, word) but their bodies were not recovered in the disassembly excerpt and so appear as placeholders.
notable_control_flow: U8P_FGRENADE::look: read-only flow — call Item::getFrame(this) into local1, call FREE::resetRef(0x0200h, this), choose one of three barks based on frame ranges (frame < 4 => "fire gem", 4 <= frame < 8 => "chaos gem", otherwise => "unstable gem"), then suspend.
U8P_FGRENADE::use: guard: if Item::getQ(this) == 0 then Item::setQ(0x01, this) to arm; otherwise do nothing (prevents re-U8P_FGRENADE::use).

U8P_FGRENADE::use: frame-based branching — case local2 == 0 calls METHOD::func0676 (special-case), otherwise some frames trigger a multi-step setFrame + FREE::func1C19 + suspend animation loop, and frame == 4 triggers a loop that spawns multiple fragments (Item::legal_create) and hurls them (Item::hurl) with random offsets/velocity and finally calls igniteChaos.
U8P_FGRENADE::use: common teardown — playSFX, createSprite at the grenade's coordinates, then Item::destroy(this).
open_questions: Exact behavior and effects of METHOD::func0676 called by the frame==0 branch — disassembly shows the call but not its semantics.
Precise meaning of the item Q field read/written by Item::getQ/Item::setQ (inferred as an 'armed/used' one-shot flag but not named in script).
Type and properties of the spawned fragment items in the chaos branch (item type id passed to Item::legal_create is not recovered here).
Role and implementation details of FREE::func1C19 used during the animation loop (inferred as a timing/visual helper).
Whether U8P_FGRENADE::hit or ::gotHit are intended to interrupt/trigger early explosion or merely log/no-op hooks (no body recovered).

main_function.name: U8P_FGRENADE::look
main_function.purpose: Provide a short, player-facing description of the grenade item based on its display frame and refresh an associated object reference/timeout.
entry_conditions: Player inspects/examines the item (engine invokes the object's U8P_FGRENADE::look process).
Engine/UI requests the item's short description or tooltip.

function_map.name: U8P_FGRENADE::look
function_map.role: inspection/display handler (reads frame, barks one-line description, resets reference)

function_map.name: U8P_FGRENADE::use
function_map.role: primary activation/one-shot behavior (arms item, runs variant-specific explosion/fragment/chaos behavior, plays SFX, creates sprites, destroys item)

function_map.name: U8P_FGRENADE::hit
function_map.role: hook invoked when the item is U8P_FGRENADE::hit (signature present but body not recovered — placeholder)

function_map.name: U8P_FGRENADE::gotHit
function_map.role: hook invoked when the item receives damage/impact (signature present but body not recovered — placeholder)

external_calls.purpose: Read the item's display/variant frame to select the short description bark.
Reset an associated reference/timeout for the object after inspection (refresh state).
Check whether the item is already armed/used to guard against reactivation.
Mark the item as armed/used (set one-shot/armed flag) when first activated.
Obtain the variant/frame to decide which activation branch to run.
Advance displayed frame(s) during the multi-step animation sequence before explosion.
Invoke a special-case handler for a specific frame value (frame == 0) — exact effect unknown.
Spawn/queue a helper/timer process used between frame updates in the animation loop (timing/visual helper inferred).
Get world X coordinate of the grenade for spawning/hurling fragments and placing effects.
Get world Y coordinate of the grenade for spawning/hurling fragments and placing effects.
Get world Z (plane/height) coordinate for spawn/hurl and area effects (igniteChaos).
Generate signed random offsets to scatter spawned fragment positions in the chaos branch.
Generate random values used to vary hurl parameters (angle/speed components) for fragments.
Attempt to create/spawn fragment items at computed coordinates; returns an out/ref handle checked before hurling.
Launch/hurl newly created fragment items as projectiles with randomized parameters.
Trigger a wide-area chaos/ignition effect for the 'chaos' variant (frame == 4 branch).
Play explosion/activation sound effect as part of activation teardown.
Create explosion/visual effect sprites at offsets from the grenade's coordinates.
Remove/destroy the grenade item instance after activation/explosion.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the in-game behaviour for a grenade-like item. It provides a read-only 'U8P_FGRENADE::look' description depending on the item's frame/variant, and a 'U8P_FGRENADE::use' process that arms and activates the grenade producing different effects depending on the variant (animation + explosion, a special-case handler, or a multi-fragment "chaos" spread).
